tasks.description: Add SqliteWriter class to persistence/sqlite.py with insert_position() and update_position_closed() methods using INSERT OR IGNORE upsert semantics.
acceptance_criteria: SqliteWriter class exists in persistence/sqlite.py and accepts a sqlite3.Connection.
insert_position(position_id, pair) creates a new position record; duplicate position_id is silently ignored (idempotent).
update_position_closed(position_id) sets closed_at to current UTC timestamp; raises a typed error if position_id does not exist.
Both methods operate within the existing WAL-mode connection and schema from SqliteAdapter.
Tests create a real in-memory SQLite DB, call ensure_schema(), and verify insert/update/idempotency behavior.

tasks.description: Add insert_order() and insert_ledger_entry() to SqliteWriter, including the ledger table DDL in ensure_schema().
acceptance_criteria: insert_order(order_id, pair, client_order_id, position_id=None, exchange_order_id=None, recorded_fee=None) creates an order record with proper foreign key to positions.
Duplicate order_id is silently ignored (idempotent upsert).
A ledger table is added to ensure_schema() with columns: id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, pair TEXT, side TEXT, quantity TEXT, price TEXT, fee TEXT, filled_at TEXT, created_at TEXT DEFAULT current_timestamp.
insert_ledger_entry(pair, side, quantity, price, fee, filled_at) appends a row to the ledger table (append-only, no upsert).
Tests verify order insertion with and without position_id, duplicate handling, ledger append, and foreign key constraint enforcement.

tasks.description: Wire the reconciler in trading/reconciler.py to use SqliteWriter to seed the database when Kraken state contains positions/orders not yet recorded in SQLite.
acceptance_criteria: Reconciler accepts an optional SqliteWriter parameter (or gets it from the adapter).
When reconciliation finds Kraken orders not in SQLite (untracked), it calls SqliteWriter.insert_order() to seed them.
When reconciliation finds Kraken positions not in SQLite, it calls SqliteWriter.insert_position() to seed them.
Seeding only happens for orders with a cl_ord_id matching the kbv4- prefix (foreign orders are logged but not seeded).
Tests mock Kraken state with both tracked and foreign orders and verify only kbv4-prefixed orders are seeded to SQLite.
